question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: mapEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a path where mapEntityId() receives a Long source value that, when decoded via EntityId.of(source), produces a different shard/realm/num combination than the original entity ID used to query, causing the GraphQL response to present a different entity ID than the one actually stored in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: mapDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a path where mapDuration() receives a Long value representing an auto-renew period that overflows Duration.ofSeconds(), causing the serialized duration in the GraphQL response to misrepresent the historical auto-renew configuration of a Hashgraph account?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: mapContractId()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a path where mapContractId() receives a ContractID with non-zero shardNum/realmNum/contractNum that equals the default instance, causing the null check to fail and returning a non-null EntityId that misrepresents the historical contract association of a Hashgraph account?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: serialize()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a path where GraphQlTimestamp.serialize() receives an Instant with nanosecond precision that Instant.toString() truncates or rounds, causing the serialized timestamp in the GraphQL response to differ from the actual Hashgraph consensus timestamp stored in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: serialize()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a path where GraphQlDuration.serialize() receives a Duration with nanosecond components that Duration.toString() represents in a non-standard format, causing downstream consumers to misparse the duration and apply incorrect time windows when querying Hashgraph history?
